page: Microsoft Azure D64d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 2458 GB local storage drive.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core processing, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is highly efficient, reaching 291.88 GB/sec for read operations and 134.9 GB/sec for write operations. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 62.01 million operations per second and static web serving throughput peaking at 51.52 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core benchmarks, including single-threaded gzip compression and single-core Geekbench scores, fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64d_v5 offers a high density of compute and memory resources combined with substantial bundled local storage, which reduces the need for external block storage attachments for temporary data. This hardware profile is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is less optimal for workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration or those constrained strictly by single-core clock speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2458 GB local storage drive, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth for large blocks, and excellent database throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 62.01 million operations per second. Single-core performance and LLM inference speeds remain in the average tier. This server provides a balanced resource configuration, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64d_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial bundled local storage, and strong multi-core performance for database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio.
Bundled 2458 GB of local storage for high-speed temporary data handling.
Strong multi-core performance in stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark benchmarks.
High-throughput database capabilities, achieving 62.01 million Redis SET operations per second.
Appropriate for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and web serving.
